question.text: A USDINR 82 call is priced at Rs. 1.20 when spot is 83.20. It is:

answer.text: In the Money with intrinsic value of Rs. 1.20 and minimal time value
Cannot determine moneyness from given information

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Spot (83.20) > Strike (82) → Call is ITM. Intrinsic = 83.20 - 82 = 1.20. Premium = 1.20, so time value = 1.20 - 1.20 = 0 (or very small). This is a deep ITM call with delta near 1. **The Trap:** Even though premium = intrinsic (near-zero time value), the option is ITM, not ATM. **Cross-Exam Context:** Deep ITM option characteristics are tested in Series I. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Deep ITM options behave like the underlying itself (delta ≈ 1). They have minimal time value decay.
